calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-08-2016 Off Calendar

UNINSURED MOTORIST ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-23-2015 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Feb-23-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Compel Production Of Audio Recording Of Medical Examination
Off Calendar Nov-03-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MARK CARBONE, ESQ. of Law Offices of Carbone, Smoke, Smith, Bent & Leonard, Tel No. 510-267-7270 appearing for Respondent CSAA Insurance Exchange;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - Respondent's counsel stipulates to matter being heard by the pro tem judge. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Respondent's Motion to Compel Production of Audio Recording of Medical Examination is granted. Plaintiff's argument that the audio recording is work product is not persuasive. Coito held that the work product doctrine protects "witness statements obtained as a result of an interview conducted by an attorney or by an attorney's agent at the attorney's behest." (Coito v. Superior Court, 54 Cal.4th 480, 494 (2012).) The audio recording at issue here was not obtained as a result of an interview conducted by Plaintiff's counsel or at her counsel's behest. Furthermore, the audio recording is within the scope of discovery, and the statute does not prohibit its production. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem: Anthony Schoenberg, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Audio Recording Of Medical Examination
Off Calendar Oct-26-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
RESPONDENT CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE's Motion To Compel Production Of Audio Recording Of Medical Examination is transferred to Department 301 to be heard on the same date at the same time. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =(302/EHG)

Motion To Compel Production Of Audio Recording Of Medical Examination
